primary_reason: The questionnaire describes healthcare workers' needlestick practices and attitudes using standard descriptive statistics and regression without developing a transferable measurement or surveillance method.
The review summarizes preclinical neuropathology in infected animals without a transferable epidemiological method contribution.
The review combines Belgian laboratory and mandatory-notification data to expose surveillance under-ascertainment and assess regulatory and diagnostic gaps for psittacosis.
The study validates maternal vaccination recall against records using agreement, bias, sensitivity, specificity, and predictor analyses across five vaccines.
The study links hepatitis C laboratory surveillance to death registrations to measure mortality, quantify death-certificate underreporting, and monitor treatment-era changes.
This case-based review concerns immune-checkpoint-inhibitor renal toxicity in cancer and does not provide a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
The study nests measles and rubella serology within a campaign coverage survey to compare reported vaccination with measured immunity and identify otherwise hidden immunity gaps.
The ecological time-series analysis corrects childhood mortality for ill-defined causes and death underreporting while separately tracking vaccine-preventable and other cause groups.
The audit verifies children classified as overdue in the Australian Immunisation Register and quantifies coverage errors caused by missing and duplicate records.
The decision-tree analysis estimates vaccination impact and economic value under sensitivity scenarios.
The review addresses smoking, alcohol, and diabetes, with infections mentioned only as one component of alcohol-related morbidity and no concrete infectious-disease method.
The study develops imaging flow cytometry to distinguish active from viable-but-nonculturable Pseudomonas aeruginosa and experimentally demonstrates amoeba-associated resuscitation.
The study validates parental reports of HPV-vaccine-naive status against electronic health records and models predictors of accurate reporting.
The study describes clinical and laboratory manifestations among patients meeting an expanded-dengue definition without contributing a reusable design or measurement method.

The spontaneous-report case series describes Fournier gangrene associated with diabetes medicines but does not evaluate or correct the reporting system or establish a transferable epidemiologic method.
The mechanistic transmission model represents hospital, community, infant, asymptomatic-carrier, and animal sources to estimate Clostridium difficile transmission and case misclassification.
The study models antibody kinetics to derive seroincidence measurements that reduce case-underreporting bias.
The narrative review summarizes Borrelia miyamotoi ecology, clinical presentation, diagnosis, and knowledge gaps without substantively developing or comparing epidemiologic methods.
The study combines population serology, outbreak testing, and sentinel fever-rash surveillance to reveal varicella, measles, and rubella misdiagnosis and underreporting.
The report describes one daptomycin-resistant Corynebacterium striatum infection and its mutations without a transferable surveillance or analytic design.
The study compiles donor-screening results, transfusion-transmitted infections, and national interventions to assess West Nile and Usutu virus blood-safety surveillance in Europe.
The case-control analysis concerns diet and central nervous system demyelination, and adjustment for infectious mononucleosis does not provide an infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
The systematic review evaluates reconstructive surgical missions and follow-up quality without an infectious-disease outcome or concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The retrospective student survey describes bloodborne-pathogen exposure reporting and reasons for nonreporting without evaluating a reusable measurement or correction method.
The report describes vaccination coverage, supplementary activities, and reported measles incidence in Pakistan without introducing a transferable analytic or surveillance-adjustment method.
The study matches clinically confirmed hepatitis B and C records to state surveillance records to quantify underreporting and identify affected patient groups.
The study evaluates a transmission-model and data-assimilation forecasting framework and tests backcasting and search-trend nowcasting as corrections for influenza surveillance errors.
The study applies standardized data-quality self-assessment tools to audit the accuracy, completeness, timeliness, and use of reported immunization data.
The review summarizes African Campylobacter prevalence and antimicrobial resistance but does not substantively compare or prescribe epidemiologic methods.
The mixed-methods survey describes needlestick knowledge and reporting barriers among junior doctors without developing a transferable measurement or surveillance method.
The study diagnoses outcome-reporting bias in a randomized trial by validating self-reported repeat pregnancy against vital-record births.
The study describes reported congenital cytomegalovirus cases and their association with maternal infection type but does not directly estimate or correct surveillance completeness.
The burden-of-disease analysis adjusts registered child deaths for underreporting, HIV misclassification, ill-defined causes, and injury data to reconstruct cause-specific mortality trends.
The commentary provides concrete system-design guidance for replacing incomplete passive vaccine-safety surveillance with active surveillance.

The study evaluates measurement of contraceptive withdrawal and ejaculation location, while sexually transmitted infection prevention is only a counseling consideration rather than the measured outcome.
The analysis uses national mortality data to estimate fatal congenital Zika syndrome deaths missed by the existing surveillance system.
The claims analysis concerns injuries from central venous access devices, and infection is only one complication within a non-infectious patient-safety outcome.
The study compares health-insurance records with mandatory syphilis notifications to quantify underreporting and propose an additional surveillance source.
The study combines surveillance, hospital-cost, employment, and longitudinal outcome data with alternative underreporting assumptions to estimate chikungunya outbreak burden.
The review pools state validation studies to estimate sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, and classification errors in national catheter-associated urinary-tract-infection reporting.
The study applies a transferable cross-sectional serosurvey and reservoir-linked risk-factor design.
The analysis describes demographic and geographic influenza-mortality disparities using routine data but does not correct the acknowledged racial misclassification or underreporting.
The study estimates underreporting of snakebite envenomation, which is outside infectious diseases and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The literature review summarizes Caribbean leptospirosis epidemiology, serovars, and surveillance gaps without substantively evaluating or prescribing a method.
The case-control analysis concerns fish intake and demyelination, and adjustment for infectious mononucleosis does not create a concrete infectious-disease method.
The study applies a WHO routine-data-quality tool to verify immunization and reproductive-health indicators for completeness, discrepancy, underreporting, and overreporting.
The field assessment matches records across anonymous HIV testing and care sites to identify linkage-monitoring failures and specify identifiers and protocols that reduce underreporting.
The pilot develops and tests a FHIR-based application that automatically extracts electronic-record information for sexually transmitted infection case reporting.
The mechanistic epidemic model represents human, vector, primary-host, and alternative-host transmission and estimates cutaneous-leishmaniasis reporting completeness and control effects.
The study develops and internally evaluates a clinical scoring model for presumptive leptospirosis diagnosis when confirmatory testing is unavailable.
The study combines burden models, spatial mapping, and an ensemble of transmission models to estimate underreported dengue and the prospective impact of nationwide Wolbachia deployment.
The study links hepatitis C laboratory surveillance with hospital admissions to monitor severe liver outcomes and quantify incomplete HCV coding.
The study uses an implant-retrieval registry to compare temporal distributions of prosthetic joint infection and diagnose follow-up-related undercounting in clinical studies.
The study augments surgical-site-infection surveillance with discharge, microbiology, and emergency data followed by targeted review to recover missed infections.
The machine-learning analysis corrects suicide classification among drug-overdose deaths and provides no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study uses capture-recapture, linked notification and hospital records, and geographic extrapolation to estimate underreported tick-borne encephalitis.
The global analysis combines surveillance completeness, incidence, vaccination status, and age distributions to interpret diphtheria patterns under uneven reporting.
The study links three HIV registers and applies log-linear capture-recapture with a mortality correction to estimate the infected population and registry ascertainment.
The study validates self-reported alcohol use with a biomarker among women living with HIV, but alcohol use rather than an infectious outcome is the measurement target.
The protocol substantively specifies an integrated diarrheal-surveillance method combining point-process anomaly detection, targeted sampling, genomic diagnostics, and cost-utility analysis.
The systematic review audits reporting of twelve key offspring outcomes in pregnancy trials, including HIV trials, and documents reproducibility-limiting omissions.
The study appraises rabies and animal-bite surveillance by comparing records across hospitals, state programs, and national sources for concordance and underreporting.

The outbreak investigation combines active case finding, interviews, environmental assessment, microbiology, and whole-genome sequencing to evaluate person-to-person Salmonella transmission and symptom underreporting.
The cohort links hepatitis C laboratory reports to the transplant registry to estimate transplant incidence, post-transplant survival, and registry underreporting.
The study compares single and linked administrative sources and alternative follow-up windows to quantify under-ascertainment of surgical-site infections after arthroplasty.
The review concerns opioid-induced endocrine disorders and supplies no infectious-disease outcome or concrete transfer route.
The mixed-methods study describes child mortality, recent illnesses, and cultural perceptions in rural Nepal without developing a reusable infectious-disease measurement method.
The study evaluates headman and household reports of foot-and-mouth disease against serology using latent-class models to estimate reporting sensitivity and specificity.

The paper develops a consistent nonparametric competing-risks estimator that corrects differential outcome misclassification using external validation data and propagates validation uncertainty by bootstrap.
The study evaluates calibration choices for an age-structured SEIR influenza model and shows how contact structure, baselines, and underreporting information affect inferred parameters.
The interview study primarily measures childhood sexual experiences, psychological distress, and substance use, with HIV status only a participant characteristic.
The study links neurologic registries with measles surveillance and applies alternative reporting-completeness assumptions to estimate subacute sclerosing panencephalitis risk.
The provider survey describes coccidioidomycosis knowledge and reporting awareness without validating a surveillance measure or correction procedure.
The surgical database analysis concerns parotidectomy outcomes and reconstruction, with surgical-site infection only one postoperative complication.

The HIV-prevention program assessment combines clinical records, antibiotic dispensing, national reports, and direct postoperative observation to recover unreported circumcision adverse events, including infections.
The report presents three neonatal Elizabethkingia infection or colonization cases without evaluating a transferable diagnostic or surveillance method.
The study evaluates a Buruli-ulcer surveillance system against CDC attributes, including sensitivity, predictive value, data quality, timeliness, stability, and underreporting.
The study probabilistically links drug-resistant-tuberculosis and antiretroviral registers to diagnose missing, discordant, and underreported treatment and mortality data.
The analysis measures depression and psychosocial correlates among participants in an HIV pre-exposure-prophylaxis trial rather than an infectious outcome or infectious surveillance process.
The study compares hospital tuberculosis records with national reports and uses interviews to diagnose the magnitude and organizational causes of underreporting.
This preclinical antigen-development study predicts and validates epitopes using immunized mouse sera.
The review concerns immune-mediated post-transfusion purpura and does not identify a concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The paper develops a multilayer dynamic hepatitis C transmission model that represents disease, injecting, diagnosis, and acquisition states and evaluates treatment and needle-exchange interventions.
The paper provides transferable guidance for disease detection, reporting, contingency planning, and surveillance-system design.
The study compares norovirus incidence across insurance, ambulatory, inpatient, and statutory surveillance sources to diagnose missed notification and misclassification.
The study evaluates culture-guided prophylaxis and rectal screening as strategies for predicting and preventing sepsis.

The pharmacovigilance analysis profiles serious adverse reactions in a national database in which HIV and antiretroviral medicines predominate, providing a transferable safety-surveillance design for infectious-disease treatment programs.
The study audits laboratory testing during reported gastroenteritis outbreaks to show how notification rules and limited diagnostics conceal norovirus cases.
The study develops and compares machine-learning methods for detecting consumer reports of unsafe foods, including pathogen-contaminated products relevant to foodborne-disease surveillance.
The early-outbreak analysis fits an exponential epidemic model by maximum likelihood to estimate unreported coronavirus cases, changing reporting rates, and the reproduction number.
The review assembles published and unpublished foodborne-outbreak records and audits available investigation reports for epidemiologic, laboratory, and food-production deficiencies.
This abstractless published erratum does not state the corrected content and therefore adds no independently screenable method, diagnostic, or guidance contribution.
The feasibility study tests an application prototype and elicits user preferences for mobile reporting of adverse events following vaccination.
Comparing external signs with histology directly quantifies diagnostic underascertainment of parasitic infection.
The study applies a randomized double-list experiment to estimate condom use among sex workers without direct-question social-desirability bias and compares the resulting HIV-risk estimates.
The systematic review catalogs equine zoonotic pathogens, transmission routes, and risk factors without substantively evaluating an epidemiologic method.
The study measures occupational ultraviolet exposure for skin-cancer prevention and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study integrates microbiology, hospital, death-registry, and notification data to recover melioidosis cases and deaths largely absent from national surveillance.
The review chapter summarizes Buruli-ulcer history, burden, evolution, transmission uncertainty, and pathogenesis without developing or evaluating a transferable method.
The data article documents a population cohort with baseline and annual serology, weekly fever surveillance, home sampling, and confirmatory testing for reproducible dengue-incidence estimation.
The study evaluates Bayesian adjustment with Gibbs sampling to show how assumed hepatitis B underreporting and misclassification alter estimated vaccination effects.
The review summarizes immune evasion and pathogenesis in Mycobacterium ulcerans and M. leprae without a transferable epidemiologic-method contribution.
The study predicts opioid involvement in unclassified overdose deaths and supplies no concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
The survey describes needlestick incidence, nonreporting, and opinions about high-risk announcements without evaluating the proposed intervention or a reusable surveillance method.
This nonresearch study guide summarizes lipoid pneumonia diagnosis and management but contributes no infectious-disease method, diagnostic evaluation, or reproducibility resource.
The review evaluates laboratory-acquired-infection evidence and biosafety guidance and prescribes structured reporting and epidemiologic surveillance for research laboratories.
The study sequentially corrects reported chlamydia rates for incomplete screening, diagnostic misclassification, and underreporting to show how these biases distort temporal trends.
The study uses an age-specific cost-effectiveness model and sensitivity analyses to evaluate adult pertussis vaccination under uncertain burden, effectiveness, costs, and underreporting.
The review concerns asbestos-related cancers and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiologic methods.
The hospital analysis primarily describes opioid-use admissions, with infections only a secondary discharge category rather than the surveillance outcome.
The qualitative study uses expert interviews and notification data to diagnose resource, workflow, and private-sector integration weaknesses in dengue surveillance.
The review addresses chronic obstructive pulmonary disease assessment and treatment, with respiratory infections mentioned only as exacerbation triggers.
The analysis corrects vital-registration deaths for incomplete reporting and uses excess-mortality regression to reassign misattributed HIV deaths before evaluating antiretroviral-therapy-era trends.

The clinic-record study reports associations between nationality, economic period, and sexually transmitted infection diagnoses using standard cross-sectional analyses without a reusable design contribution.
The surveillance analysis describes healthcare workers experiencing biological-material accidents but neither measures underreporting nor evaluates a correction or reporting intervention.
The systematic review summarizes reported chikungunya circulation in Africa but the abstract identifies no substantive comparison or prescription of methods.
The reproductive-age mortality survey estimates maternal deaths and their causes, with infection only one obstetric cause rather than an infectious-disease methodological target.
The study develops and evaluates machine-learning surgical-site-infection classifiers that integrate natural-language-processing features with structured electronic-record data.
The paper adapts ecological distance sampling to estimate distance-dependent infectious-case reporting and provides an interactive implementation demonstrated with malaria clinic data.
The household survey estimates induced abortion and complications, with possible infection being a procedural complication rather than an infectious-disease outcome.
The study models sylvatic and urban yellow-fever transmission, calibrates underreporting with serosurveys, and forecasts burden, vaccine demand, herd effects, and campaign impact.

The historical epidemiologic study triangulates medical reports, government records, newspapers, and private documents to reconstruct a severely underreported measles epidemic and its social determinants.
The article comments on conflict-related vulnerabilities and proposes broad COVID-19 capacity improvements in Syria without presenting a substantive analytic method.
The modeling synthesis examines how stochastic transmission, diagnostic error, underreporting, reservoirs, spatial scale, and intervention coverage affect inference of trypanosomiasis elimination.
The study uses time-varying mathematical models with inverse and forward uncertainty quantification and sensitivity analysis to infer COVID-19 mitigation and underreporting across three countries.
The cross-sectional pharmacy survey describes COVID-19 preparedness and reporting behavior without evaluating a transferable surveillance intervention or correction method.
The study applies centralized pharmacovigilance review to estimate infection-treatment adverse-event incidence.
The study combines laboratory and multisector surveillance data to assess rabies underreporting and surveillance capacity.
The review translates evidence on Rift Valley fever underdiagnosis, exposure, clinical severity, and vaccine limitations into risk-mitigation and outbreak-preparedness guidance.
The questionnaire estimates occupational blood-and-body-fluid exposures and reasons for nonreporting but does not evaluate a reporting intervention or correction method.
The study develops a model-based inference approach that controls for underreporting and changing surveillance intensity when estimating COVID-19 growth, reproduction, and intervention requirements.
The analysis uses adjusted case-fatality calculations and cross-country comparisons to infer the scale of undetected and underreported COVID-19 infections.
An age-structured decision model compares integrated dengue interventions and parameter uncertainty.
The study extends an analytical re-emergence estimator and diagnoses aggregated SIR-model limitations through stochastic simulation.
The registry analysis estimates overall cancer mortality, and its mention of vaccine-preventable cancers does not establish an infectious-disease measurement contribution.
The review develops bioinformatic strategies for hereditary-cancer splice variants and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The incident-report analysis concerns general pediatric medication errors, with antimicrobials and vaccines only two drug categories within a non-infectious patient-safety outcome.
The paper specifies and validates a decentralized rapid postmortem rabies test protocol that preserves material for safe confirmatory molecular typing.
The review provides ecosystem-specific guidance for integrating surveillance, diagnostics, vaccination, and control decisions.
The expert survey evaluates the feasibility and perceived value of surgical-site-infection quality indicators and identifies requirements for valid claims-based surveillance.
The study combines surveillance analysis, literature review, and workload measurement to evaluate whether mandatory apartment water monitoring prevents legionellosis.
The cohort primarily evaluates colorectal-surgery readmissions, with deep infection only one cause of later readmission rather than the methodological target.
The narrative review systematically searches and summarizes meningococcal burden, serogroups, and vaccination recommendations without substantively evaluating an epidemiologic method.
The pilot develops an integrated surveillance system specifically for noncommunicable diseases and provides no concrete infectious-disease implementation or validation.
The study fits mortality time-series regressions to historical government data and uses excess respiratory deaths to estimate underreported COVID-19 mortality.
The article discusses examples and possible reasons for COVID-19 underreporting in Turkey without presenting a substantive estimation or diagnostic method.
The historical review summarizes canine parvovirus epidemiology, surveillance, and knowledge gaps in Australia without substantively evaluating a reusable method.
The prospective study evaluates inter-rater reliability, feasibility, and user experience for a web-based community surgical-site-infection surveillance tool.
The report describes declining measles notifications during the COVID-19 pandemic and raises possible underdiagnosis without estimating or correcting it.
The study institutionalizes repeated data-quality assessments using malaria register-to-database verification factors and demonstrates improved integrated-surveillance accuracy over time.
